forest-g3.title: Forest Clearing
text: A clearing opens in the dark wood, wrapped in silver fog that drifts but never fully settles.
A small pond sits at the centre. Something moves beneath its surface — a fish, watching.
To the north, the fog thickens. Something shifts in it. Probably nothing.

pond.roomDesc: The pond is still except where it isn't. The fish knows you're here.
pond.lookText: Clear enough to see the bottom. The fish is down there, doing slow circles, absolutely judging you.

fog.roomDesc: A wall of fog rolls in slowly from the north, thicker than it should be.

lookTextByRace.goblin: You squint into it. Something moves. Low. Patient. You know that shape. You've been that shape.
lookTextByRace.human: You stare into the fog. Shadows shift where shadows shouldn't. Something is in there, waiting for a reason to come out.
lookTextByRace.elf: The fog has texture. Intent, almost. Something breathes inside it that isn't the wind.

sign.roomDesc: A weathered wooden sign leans against a tree at the pond's edge.
sign.lookText:
Old carving, deliberate hand:

+-----------------+
| DON'T THROW     |
| THINGS.         |
| it scares       |
| the fish.       |
|   - the fish    |
+-----------------+

'Management' is scratched out.

roomDescByRace.goblin: A small mouse has crept out of the bushes. It freezes when it sees you, vibrating with indecision.
roomDescByRace.human: A mouse scurries out from the undergrowth and pauses, nose twitching, pretending it meant to be here.
roomDescByRace.elf: A mouse has emerged from the brush. It sits very still, as if stillness will make it invisible.

lookTextByRace.goblin: Tiny. Fast. Probably delicious. It's looking at you like it's already planning its escape route.
lookTextByRace.human: A small brown mouse. Alert eyes, twitching whiskers. It's deciding whether you're dangerous.
lookTextByRace.elf: A mouse. Small life, large awareness. It knows exactly where you are.

noticeByRace.goblin: The mouse locks eyes with you. Every muscle in it tenses.
noticeByRace.human: The mouse sees you properly for the first time. It goes very still.
noticeByRace.elf: The mouse becomes aware of you. The clearing feels smaller.

approachByRace.goblin: It decides you're a threat. Fast little legs, faster than they look.
approachByRace.human: It darts toward you — cornered, or just stupid.
approachByRace.elf: It moves. Quickly. More intent than you expected from something so small.

meleeByRace.goblin: It's on your boot. Tiny teeth, real commitment.
meleeByRace.human: It's biting your ankle. This is embarrassing for both of you.
meleeByRace.elf: It engages you with more dignity than the situation warrants.

hitByRace.human: 🐭 The mouse bites for {dmg}. ({hp} hp left.)
hitByRace.elf: 🐭 A small wound. {dmg} damage. ({hp} hp remaining.)

missByRace.goblin: 🐭 It snaps at air. You're faster than a mouse, at least.

deathByRace.goblin: It drops. You feel something. You're not sure what.
deathByRace.human: The mouse goes still. The clearing feels quieter.
deathByRace.elf: It falls. A small life, ended. The pond doesn't care.

rock.roomText: A smooth rock sits half-buried in the mud at the water's edge.

msgByRace.goblin: The rock hits the water with a satisfying crack. The fish explodes out, absolutely furious, and spits something shiny at your feet before disappearing in a huff.
msgByRace.human: The rock splashes in. A fish leaps out — apparently offended — deposits something at your feet with great indignation, and vanishes.
msgByRace.elf: The stone breaks the stillness. The fish rises, takes one look at you, leaves something at your feet with quiet contempt, and is gone.

ambientTexts: The fish stirs the surface of the pond. Once. Then stops.
Something moves in the fog to the north. You tell yourself it's the wind.
The fog breathes. In. Out. Almost.
A ripple crosses the pond from no visible source.
The fish breaks the surface, looks directly at you, and submerges again.
The silver fog shifts. For a moment, something in it has a shape.
Silence, except for the water. And something that might be breathing.
The sign creaks. There is no wind.
